normalized_definitions.surface_preparation_and_chemical_treatme: Mechanical or chemical treatments applied to the sample prior to testing, such as pickling, electropolishing, or sandblasting, to remove cold-worked layers and modify surface defect density or chemistry.

normalized_definitions.specimen_geometry_and_dimensions: The physical shape and precise dimensions of the test electrode or specimen (e.g., rod diameter, sheet thickness, or coupon size), which define the exposed surface area and influence edge effects.

normalized_definitions.specimen_surface_finish: The final mechanical abrasive, polishing, or grinding treatment applied to the specimen surface (e.g., specific grit size, material, or slurry), which dictates surface roughness and localized corrosion initiation sites.

normalized_definitions.crevice_former_specification: The material, geometry, and design parameters of the device used to artificially induce crevice corrosion on the specimen surface, such as specific TFE-fluorocarbon washers.
